name: Account Created Confirmation
summary: A transactional email confirming a new account has been created. Sets expectations for what comes next and establishes early trust with new users.

name: Activity Update
summary: Automated notifications summarizing recent activity or events triggered by user actions. Keeps users informed about what's happening in their account and drives return visits.

name: Affiliate Partnership
summary: Emails promoting affiliate or partner program opportunities. Encourages existing users to refer others in exchange for commissions or rewards, expanding the customer base organically.

name: Article
summary: Content-driven emails sharing articles, blog posts, or editorial pieces. Used to educate, build authority, and keep subscribers engaged between product touchpoints.

name: Beta Testing
summary: Invitations to test new features before they launch publicly. Rewards engaged users with early access while generating valuable product feedback ahead of a broader rollout.

name: Billing Reminder
summary: Proactive reminders about upcoming charges, plan renewals, or payment due dates. Reduces payment failures and involuntary churn by giving users advance notice.

name: Case Study
summary: Emails highlighting real customer success stories and measurable results. Build social proof and help prospects or users envision similar outcomes with the product.

name: Complete Purchase
summary: Nudges sent to users who started but did not finish a purchase or upgrade flow. Recovers abandoned conversions by reducing friction and reinforcing the value proposition.

name: Conference Invite
summary: Invitations to industry conferences, summits, or brand-hosted events. Strengthens community ties and positions the brand as a thought leader in its space.

name: Content Engagement
summary: Emails designed to drive engagement with specific content — blog posts, webinars, guides, or videos. Keeps users active and deepens their connection to the brand between logins.

name: Course
summary: Multi-step email sequences delivering structured educational content over time. Builds user competency and increases the likelihood of long-term product adoption.

name: Customer Success
summary: Check-in emails from customer success teams offering support, tips, and next steps. Reduces churn by proactively identifying and resolving user challenges early in the lifecycle.

name: Downgrade Receipt
summary: Confirmation emails sent when a user downgrades their plan. Acknowledges the change, clarifies which features are no longer available, and subtly reinforces the value of the higher tier.

name: Download Chrome Extension
summary: Prompts encouraging users to install the product's browser extension. Increases daily active use by embedding the product directly into the user's existing browser workflow.

name: Download Mobile App
summary: Emails nudging users to download the mobile version of the product. Extends usage beyond desktop and improves retention by making the product accessible anywhere.

name: Download Web App
summary: Prompts to access or bookmark the web app version of the product. Reduces friction for first-time access and drives users toward the core product experience.

name: eBook
summary: Emails promoting or delivering downloadable eBooks and long-form guides. Generates leads, nurtures prospects, and positions the brand as a trusted authority in its category.

name: Email Verification
summary: Transactional emails asking users to confirm their email address. A critical trust and deliverability step that ensures accounts are tied to valid, active addresses.

name: Feature Update
summary: Announcements about new or improved product features. Reminds current users of evolving value and gives inactive users a compelling reason to return and re-engage.

name: Feature Upsell
summary: Targeted emails promoting a specific paid feature or capability the user hasn't unlocked yet. Converts free or lower-tier users by showcasing tangible, relevant benefits.

name: Feature Usage Nudge
summary: Behavioral prompts sent when users haven't explored a key feature. Drives product adoption by surfacing capabilities at the moment they're most relevant to the user's journey.

name: Follow up
summary: Re-engagement emails sent after a significant action, inactivity period, or sales conversation. Maintains momentum with prospects or users and prevents opportunities from going cold.

name: Guide
summary: Step-by-step how-to emails helping users get more value from the product. Accelerates time-to-value and reduces support overhead by proactively answering common questions.

name: Inactivity Nudge
summary: Re-engagement emails triggered by a period of user inactivity. Remind dormant users of the product's value and prompt them to return before they fully churn.

name: Integration Prompt
summary: Emails encouraging users to connect third-party tools and integrations. Deeper integrations increase switching costs and make the product significantly stickier over time.

name: Interview
summary: Invitations to participate in customer interviews or user research calls. Collects qualitative insights that inform product roadmap decisions and improve retention strategies.

name: IRL Event
summary: Invitations to in-person meetups, workshops, or brand events. Deepens customer relationships beyond the digital product and builds lasting community loyalty.

name: Join Community
summary: Emails inviting users to join a Slack group, forum, or online community. Community membership increases retention by creating social accountability and peer support networks.

name: Join Waitlist
summary: Emails encouraging users to join a waitlist for a new product, feature, or plan tier. Builds anticipation and captures demand before an official launch.

name: Join Your Team
summary: Invitation emails prompting users to add teammates or colleagues to their workspace. Drives viral growth and increases stickiness by turning solo tools into team tools.

name: Login From New Device
summary: Security alerts notifying users of a login from an unrecognized device or location. Builds trust through transparency and protects accounts from unauthorized access.

name: Milestone Reached
summary: Celebratory emails triggered when a user reaches a significant usage or engagement milestone. Reinforces positive behavior, creates emotional connection, and motivates continued engagement.

name: Month in Review
summary: Monthly recap emails summarizing a user's activity, achievements, and product usage. Demonstrates ongoing value and reminds users why they subscribed in the first place.

name: New Feature(s) Nudge
summary: Targeted prompts encouraging existing users to try a newly launched feature. Bridges the gap between announcements and actual adoption by connecting the feature to the user's specific context.

name: Newsletter
summary: Regular email digests covering industry news, product updates, tips, and brand content. Maintains top-of-mind awareness and nurtures ongoing relationships with subscribers.

name: NPS Survey
summary: Net Promoter Score emails asking users how likely they are to recommend the product. A benchmark metric for measuring customer satisfaction and identifying at-risk or promoter users.

name: Nudge to Book Demo
summary: Emails encouraging trial or free users to book a personalized demo with the sales or success team. Accelerates conversion by offering direct human support at a critical decision point.

name: Nudge to Invite Team
summary: Prompts sent to solo users encouraging them to invite colleagues into the workspace. Drives account expansion and increases the overall stickiness of the product across organizations.

name: Onboarding Call Invite
summary: Emails inviting new users to schedule a live onboarding or setup call. Ensures new customers get off to a strong start and significantly reduces early-stage churn.

name: Onboarding Call Summary
summary: Follow-up emails recapping the key takeaways and next steps from an onboarding call. Reinforces commitments, provides a reference document, and keeps momentum going post-call.

name: Onboarding Checklist
summary: Structured emails guiding new users through a list of setup or activation tasks. Reduces time-to-value by breaking the onboarding journey into clear, achievable steps.

name: Onboarding Progress Nudge
summary: Reminder emails sent to users who have started but not completed their onboarding setup. Keeps new users on track and reduces early drop-off before they reach their activation moment.

name: Partner Program
summary: Emails promoting partnership, reseller, or referral program opportunities. Expands the customer network by turning satisfied users into advocates and channel partners.

name: Password Reset
summary: Transactional emails delivering a secure link to reset a forgotten password. A critical trust touchpoint — fast, clear delivery reduces friction and prevents account abandonment.

name: Payment Declined
summary: Dunning emails notifying users that their payment method has failed. Prompt, empathetic messaging helps recover failed payments and reduces involuntary churn.

name: Payment Receipt
summary: Transactional confirmation emails sent after a successful payment or subscription renewal. Builds trust, provides a paper trail, and can open the door to upsell or referral messaging.

name: Payment Reminder
summary: Proactive emails sent before a payment is due or a subscription expires. Reduces involuntary churn by giving users advance notice to ensure their payment method is current.

name: Product Education
summary: In-depth emails teaching users how to get maximum value from specific product features. Increases product competency, reduces support tickets, and drives long-term retention.

name: Product Update
summary: Changelog-style emails announcing recent improvements, fixes, and new capabilities. Demonstrates active product development and gives lapsed users a compelling reason to return.

name: Promotional Article
summary: Content marketing emails featuring thought leadership articles tied to the product's core use case. Builds authority while keeping the product top of mind for subscribers.

name: Qualitative Feedback
summary: Open-ended feedback emails asking users to describe their experience in their own words. Uncovers deeper insights about friction points and unmet needs that structured surveys miss.

name: Quota Reached
summary: Triggered emails notifying users they've hit a usage limit or plan quota. A natural upgrade moment — these emails convert free users to paid by making the plan constraint tangible.

name: Re-Engage Cancelled Users
summary: Win-back emails targeting users who have explicitly cancelled their subscription. The right offer at the right moment can recover a meaningful percentage of churned revenue.

name: Re-Engage Downgraded Users
summary: Targeted re-engagement emails for users who downgraded to a lower-tier plan. Demonstrates the premium value they're missing and aims to win back the upgrade.

name: Referral Prompt
summary: Emails encouraging satisfied users to refer friends, colleagues, or their network to the product. Referral emails convert at high rates because they come with built-in social proof.

name: Refund Policy
summary: Emails communicating the product's refund or cancellation terms. Builds trust during the purchase decision and reduces disputes by setting clear expectations upfront.

name: Report
summary: Data-driven report emails delivering personalized analytics, benchmarks, or performance summaries. Demonstrates ROI and gives users concrete proof of the value they're receiving.

name: Setup Prompt
summary: Nudges sent to users who haven't completed initial account or workspace setup. Reduces early churn by guiding users to complete the steps required to experience core product value.

name: Social Media Share
summary: Emails encouraging users to share a milestone, achievement, or piece of content on social media. Generates organic word-of-mouth and expands brand visibility through user-generated promotion.

name: Special Offer
summary: Limited-time discount or promotional emails targeting specific user segments. Creates urgency and provides a financial incentive for users to upgrade, renew, or return.

name: Survey
summary: General-purpose survey emails collecting structured feedback on product satisfaction, feature preferences, or user needs. Informs roadmap decisions and makes customers feel heard.

name: Team Invite Accepted
summary: Notification emails confirming a teammate has joined the workspace. Reinforces collaborative usage and prompts the inviting user to take the next step in onboarding their team.

name: Terms of Use Update
summary: Compliance emails notifying users of changes to terms of service or privacy policy. Required for legal compliance and an opportunity to reinforce transparency and brand trust.

name: Thank You - Download
summary: Follow-up emails sent after a user downloads a resource like an ebook, report, or template. Delivers the promised content and opens a nurture sequence to convert the lead.

name: Trial Expiration Warning
summary: Urgency-driving emails sent as a free trial approaches its end date. One of the highest-converting email types in SaaS — timing, personalization, and a clear CTA are critical.

name: Trial Expired - Upgrade
summary: Post-trial emails sent after a free trial has ended without conversion. Last-chance messaging designed to recover users who were close to upgrading before their trial lapsed.

name: Trial Extension Offer
summary: Emails offering users an extension of their free trial, often in exchange for completing a demo or specific action. Keeps prospects engaged longer and gives teams more time to convert.

name: Trial Just Started
summary: Welcome emails sent immediately when a user begins their free trial. Sets expectations, delivers the first activation nudge, and kicks off the onboarding email sequence.

name: Upgrade CTA
summary: Emails with a primary call-to-action focused on plan upgrades. Deployed across multiple lifecycle touchpoints, these are the backbone of SaaS expansion revenue.

name: Upgrade to Annual
summary: Emails promoting the switch from monthly to annual billing, typically with a discount incentive. Improves LTV and reduces voluntary churn by locking customers into longer commitments.

name: Usage Expansion
summary: Emails encouraging users to expand their usage — add more seats, increase limits, or unlock additional capacity. Targets power users who are approaching or exceeding their current plan thresholds.

name: Usage Summary
summary: Personalized emails showing users how they've used the product over a specific period. Demonstrates ongoing value and surfaces a natural moment to highlight upgrade opportunities.

name: Video
summary: Emails featuring video content — product walkthroughs, tutorials, or thought leadership. Video-led emails see higher engagement rates and communicate complex ideas more effectively than text alone.

name: Video Series
summary: Multi-episode video email sequences delivering structured educational content over time. Builds a committed audience, deepens product knowledge, and improves long-term retention.

name: Webinar Confirmation
summary: Transactional confirmation emails sent after a user registers for a webinar or virtual event. Includes event details and calendar links to maximize attendance rates.

name: Webinar Invitation
summary: Promotional emails inviting users and prospects to attend a live webinar or virtual event. An effective mid-funnel touchpoint that combines education with direct product exposure.

name: Webinar Recording
summary: Post-event follow-up emails sharing a recording of a completed webinar. Extends the reach of live events and delivers value to registrants who couldn't attend in real time.

name: Welcome - Free Users
summary: The first email a free plan user receives after signing up. Sets the tone for the entire user relationship — great welcome emails activate users faster and reduce early-stage churn.

name: Welcome - Paid Users
summary: Onboarding emails sent to users immediately after their first paid conversion. Reinforces the purchase decision, sets expectations for success, and starts the customer success journey.

name: Year in Review
summary: Annual personalized recap emails celebrating what a user has accomplished with the product over the past year. One of the highest-engagement email types in SaaS — builds loyalty and drives social sharing.
